ch01.title: Linear State Update x⁺ = Ax + Bu
ch01.prompt:
The core discrete linear model is x⁺ = A x + B u. For 2×2 A, length-2 B and scalar input u,

Implement `lin_step(A, B, x, u)` returning the next state (length-2).
ch01.starter:
def lin_step(A, B, x, u):
    pass

ch01.title: Discretize a Scalar System
ch01.prompt:
Continuous ẋ = a x + b u sampled with zero-order hold over dt becomes x⁺ = a_d x + b_d u with

    a_d = e^{a·dt},   b_d = b·(e^{a·dt} − 1)/a

Implement `c2d_scalar(a, b, dt)` → [a_d, b_d] (assume a ≠ 0).
ch01.solution:
import math

def c2d_scalar(a, b, dt):
    ad = math.exp(a*dt)
    bd = b*(math.exp(a*dt) - 1)/a
    return [ad, bd]

ch01.title: State Constraints (Box)
ch01.prompt: Constraints X are often a box: lo ≤ x ≤ hi componentwise. Implement `in_box(lo, hi, x)` → bool.

ch01.title: Quadratic Form xᵀQx
ch01.prompt: The LQ cost is built from quadratic forms xᵀQx = Σᵢ Σⱼ xᵢ Qᵢⱼ xⱼ. Implement `quad_form(Q, x)`.

ch01.title: One Riccati Backward Step
ch01.prompt:
The dynamic-programming (Riccati) recursion for the scalar LQ cost-to-go P is

    P⁻ = q + a²P − (aPb)²/(r + b²P)

Implement `riccati_step(P, a, b, q, r)`.
ch01.starter:
def riccati_step(P, a, b, q, r):
    pass
ch01.solution:
def riccati_step(P, a, b, q, r):
    return q + a*a*P - (a*P*b)**2/(r + b*b*P)

ch01.title: Infinite-Horizon LQR Gain
ch01.prompt:
Iterate the Riccati recursion to its fixed point P, then the optimal gain of x⁺ = a x + b u (cost Σ q x² + r u²) is K = bPa/(r + b²P), u = −Kx.

Implement `lqr_gain_scalar(a, b, q, r)`.
ch01.starter:
def lqr_gain_scalar(a, b, q, r):
    pass

ch01.title: Controllability Matrix
ch01.prompt:
For (A, b) with 2×2 A the controllability matrix is 𝒞 = [b  Ab] (columns).

Implement `ctrb2(A, B)` returning the 2×2 matrix [[b₀, (Ab)₀], [b₁, (Ab)₁]].

ch01.title: Is the System Controllable?
ch01.prompt: (A, b) is controllable iff det[b  Ab] ≠ 0. Implement `is_controllable(A, B)` → bool.

ch01.title: Closed-Loop Stability (Scalar)
ch01.prompt:
The LQR closed loop x⁺ = (a − bK)x converges iff |a − bK| < 1.

Implement `is_stable_cl(a, b, k)` → bool.
ch01.solution:
def is_stable_cl(a, b, k):
    return abs(a - b*k) < 1

ch01.title: Gaussian Density
ch01.prompt:
State estimation assumes Gaussian noise. The 1-D density is

    p(x) = 1/(σ√(2π)) · exp(−½((x−μ)/σ)²)

Implement `gaussian_pdf(x, mu, sigma)`.

ch01.title: Least-Squares Line Fit
ch01.prompt:
Optimal estimation is rooted in least squares. Fit y = m x + c by the normal equations

    m = (nΣxy − ΣxΣy)/(nΣx² − (Σx)²),   c = (Σy − mΣx)/n

Implement `least_squares_1d(xs, ys)` → [m, c].
ch01.solution:
def least_squares_1d(xs, ys):
    n = len(xs); sx = sum(xs); sy = sum(ys)
    sxx = sum(x*x for x in xs)
    sxy = sum(xs[i]*ys[i] for i in range(n))
    m = (n*sxy - sx*sy)/(n*sxx - sx*sx)
    return [m, (sy - m*sx)/n]

ch01.title: Is the System Observable?
ch01.prompt:
With output y = C x, the pair (A, C) is observable iff det[[C],[CA]] ≠ 0 (C a length-2 row).

Implement `is_observable(A, C)` → bool.

ch01.title: Steady-State Input for a Setpoint
ch01.prompt:
To hold x at a setpoint x_sp for x⁺ = a x + b u, the steady input solves x_sp = a x_sp + b u_ss, so u_ss = (1 − a)x_sp/b.

Implement `target_input(a, b, xsp)`.
ch01.solution:
def target_input(a, b, xsp):
    return (1 - a)*xsp/b

ch02.title: MPC Stage Cost ℓ(x,u)
ch02.prompt:
The regulation stage cost is ℓ(x,u) = xᵀQx + uᵀRu. Given matrices Q, R and vectors x, u,

Implement `stage_cost(Q, R, x, u)`.
ch02.starter:
def stage_cost(Q, R, x, u):
    pass

ch02.title: Finite-Horizon Cost of a Trajectory
ch02.prompt:
The MPC objective over a horizon (scalar states/inputs) is

    J = Σₖ (q xₖ² + r uₖ²) + p_f x_N²

`xs` has N+1 states, `us` has N inputs. Implement `traj_cost(xs, us, q, r, pf)`.
ch02.starter:
def traj_cost(xs, us, q, r, pf):
    pass

ch02.title: Receding Horizon: Apply First Move
ch02.prompt:
MPC solves for a whole input sequence but *applies only the first input*, then re-optimizes.

Implement `first_control(useq)`.

ch02.title: Positive Definite Penalty (2×2)
ch02.prompt:
Stabilizing MPC needs Q ≻ 0. By Sylvester's criterion a symmetric 2×2 Q is PD iff Q₀₀ > 0 and det Q > 0.

Implement `is_posdef2(Q)` → bool.

ch02.title: Lyapunov Descent Condition
ch02.prompt:
The MPC value function is a Lyapunov function when it decreases by at least the stage cost:

    V(x⁺) − V(x) ≤ −ℓ(x,u)

Given Vx = V(x), Vnext = V(x⁺) and stage = ℓ, implement `lyap_decrease(Vx, Vnext, stage)` → bool.
ch02.solution:
def lyap_decrease(Vx, Vnext, stage):
    return Vnext - Vx <= -stage + 1e-9

ch02.title: Exponential Stability (Scalar)
ch02.prompt:
A scalar mode x⁺ = a x is exponentially stable iff |a| < 1.

Implement `is_exp_stable(a)` → bool.

ch02.title: Terminal Cost Vf(x)
ch02.prompt:
The unconstrained-LQR terminal cost is Vf(x) = p_f · xᵀx = p_f Σ xᵢ².

Implement `terminal_cost(pf, x)`.
ch02.solution:
def terminal_cost(pf, x):
    return pf*sum(xi*xi for xi in x)

ch02.title: Terminal Constraint Set Xf
ch02.prompt:
A common terminal set is the sublevel set X_f = {x : xᵀP x ≤ c}.

Implement `in_terminal_set(P, x, c)` → bool.

ch02.title: Warm-Start Shift (Suboptimal MPC)
ch02.prompt:
Suboptimal MPC warm-starts by shifting the previous input sequence: drop the applied first input and append a terminal input u_f.

Implement `shift_sequence(useq, uf)`.

ch02.title: Asymptotic Average Cost
ch02.prompt:
Economic MPC studies the average stage cost over time, (1/N)Σ ℓₖ.

Implement `average_cost(cs)` (mean of the list).

ch02.title: Discrete Actuator: Nearest Level
ch02.prompt:
With discrete actuators the input must snap to an allowed level. Return the level nearest to u (ties → the one that appears first).

Implement `quantize(u, levels)`.
ch02.solution:
def quantize(u, levels):
    return min(levels, key=lambda L: abs(L - u))

ch03.title: Inherent Robustness Margin
ch03.prompt:
A box constraint [lo, hi] tolerates a disturbance up to half its width minus the current offset. Return (hi − lo)/2 − dist.

Implement `robust_margin(box, dist)`.

ch03.title: Min-Max Robust Cost
ch03.prompt:
Min-max MPC picks the control minimizing the worst-case cost over the disturbance. `costs` is a matrix: row = control, column = disturbance. Return minᵣₒw maxᵨₗ.

Implement `worst_case_cost(costs)`.
ch03.solution:
def worst_case_cost(costs):
    return min(max(row) for row in costs)

ch03.title: One-Step Reachable Interval
ch03.prompt:
For x⁺ = a x + w with a ≥ 0, disturbance |w| ≤ W and x ∈ [lo, hi], the next state lies in

    [a·lo − W, a·hi + W]

Implement `reach_interval(a, box, w)`.
ch03.solution:
def reach_interval(a, box, w):
    return [a*box[0]-w, a*box[1]+w]

ch03.title: Minkowski Sum of Intervals
ch03.prompt:
Tubes propagate uncertainty via Minkowski sums: [a₀,a₁] ⊕ [b₀,b₁] = [a₀+b₀, a₁+b₁].

Implement `minkowski_interval(a, b)`.

ch03.title: Pontryagin Difference of Intervals
ch03.prompt:
Tube MPC erodes a set by the disturbance via the Pontryagin difference: for intervals A ⊖ B = [a₀ − b₀, a₁ − b₁].

Implement `pontry_diff(A, B)`.

ch03.title: Constraint Tightening
ch03.prompt:
Tube MPC tightens a box [lo, hi] by the tube margin m → [lo+m, hi−m].

Implement `tighten(box, m)`.

ch03.title: Invariant-Set Size (Geometric Bound)
ch03.prompt:
A rigid disturbance tube with contraction α and disturbance bound w has size after n steps

    w·(1 − αⁿ)/(1 − α)

(the partial geometric sum). Implement `mrpi_bound(alpha, w, n)`.
ch03.solution:
def mrpi_bound(alpha, w, n):
    return w*(1 - alpha**n)/(1 - alpha)

ch03.title: Support Function of a Box
ch03.prompt:
The support function of an axis-aligned box in direction d is Σᵢ max(loᵢ dᵢ, hiᵢ dᵢ).

Implement `support_box(lo, hi, d)`.

ch03.title: Inside the Tube?
ch03.prompt:
A state is inside a tube of radius R about a center (∞-norm) iff |xᵢ − centerᵢ| ≤ R for all i.

Implement `in_tube(center, radius, x)` → bool.

ch03.title: Chance-Constraint Tightening
ch03.prompt:
Stochastic MPC turns P(x ≤ b) ≥ 1−ε into a tightened deterministic bound b − k·σ (k from the quantile).

Implement `chance_tighten(bound, k, sigma)`.
ch03.solution:
def chance_tighten(bound, k, sigma):
    return bound - k*sigma

ch04.title: Kalman Gain
ch04.prompt:
The scalar Kalman gain is K = p⁻ h / (h² p⁻ + r).

Implement `kalman_gain(ppred, h, r)`.
ch04.solution:
def kalman_gain(ppred, h, r):
    return ppred*h/(h*h*ppred + r)

ch04.title: Scalar Kalman Update
ch04.prompt:
Measurement update (y = h x + noise, var r):

    K = p⁻h/(h²p⁻+r),  x⁺ = x⁻ + K(y − hx⁻),  p⁺ = (1 − Kh)p⁻

Implement `kalman_update(xpred, ppred, y, h, r)` → [x⁺, p⁺].
ch04.starter:
def kalman_update(xpred, ppred, y, h, r):
    pass

ch04.title: Covariance Prediction
ch04.prompt:
The scalar covariance time-update is p⁻ = a² p + q (q = process-noise variance).

Implement `covariance_predict(a, p, q)`.
ch04.solution:
def covariance_predict(a, p, q):
    return a*a*p + q

ch04.title: Innovation (Residual)
ch04.prompt: The innovation is e = y − h·x. Implement `innovation(y, h, x)`.
ch04.solution:
def innovation(y, h, x):
    return y - h*x

ch04.title: Weighted Squared Residual
ch04.prompt: The estimation objective sums weighted squared residuals w·e². Implement `weighted_sq(e, w)`.

ch04.title: MHE Arrival Cost
ch04.prompt:
MHE summarizes old data with an arrival cost Z(x) = ½ · pinv · (x − x̄)².

Implement `arrival_cost(x, xbar, pinv)`.
ch04.solution:
def arrival_cost(x, xbar, pinv):
    return 0.5*pinv*(x - xbar)**2

ch04.title: MHE Window Objective
ch04.prompt:
The MHE objective is the arrival cost plus the sum of stage costs in the window:

    Φ = Z + Σ ℓₖ

Implement `mhe_cost(arrival, stagecosts)`.
ch04.solution:
def mhe_cost(arrival, stagecosts):
    return arrival + sum(stagecosts)

ch04.title: Steady-State Filter Variance
ch04.prompt:
Iterate the filtering Riccati recursion to its fixed point (posterior variance p):

    p⁻ = a²p + q,   p = p⁻ − (p⁻)²h²/(h²p⁻ + r)

Implement `kf_steady_variance(a, h, q, r)`.
ch04.starter:
def kf_steady_variance(a, h, q, r):
    pass

ch05.title: Observer + Controller State Update
ch05.prompt:
Output MPC drives an estimate; the augmented (disturbance) model updates

    x⁺ = a x + b u + b_d d

Implement `augment_step(x, d, a, b, bd, u)`.
ch05.starter:
def augment_step(x, d, a, b, bd, u):
    pass
ch05.solution:
def augment_step(x, d, a, b, bd, u):
    return a*x + b*u + bd*d

ch05.title: Luenberger Observer Step
ch05.prompt:
The state estimate updates x̂⁺ = a x̂ + b u + l (y − c x̂).

Implement `luenberger_step(xhat, a, b, u, l, c, y)`.
ch05.starter:
def luenberger_step(xhat, a, b, u, l, c, y):
    pass
ch05.solution:
def luenberger_step(xhat, a, b, u, l, c, y):
    return a*xhat + b*u + l*(y - c*xhat)

ch05.title: Separation Principle: Closed-Loop Poles
ch05.prompt:
By separation, the output-feedback poles are the controller pole a − bK and the observer pole a − LC (independent).

Implement `closed_poles(a, b, k, c, l)` → [a − bK, a − LC].
ch05.starter:
def closed_poles(a, b, k, c, l):
    pass
ch05.solution:
def closed_poles(a, b, k, c, l):
    return [a - b*k, a - l*c]

ch05.title: Disturbance Estimate (Offset-Free)
ch05.prompt:
Offset-free MPC augments an integrating disturbance estimated from the output error d̂ = y − y_model.

Implement `disturbance_estimate(y, ymodel)`.
ch05.solution:
def disturbance_estimate(y, ymodel):
    return y - ymodel

ch05.title: Offset-Free Steady-State Target
ch05.prompt:
Given the estimated disturbance d, the target (x_ss, u_ss) that makes output y = c x + d equal the setpoint y_sp solves

    x_ss = (y_sp − d)/c,   u_ss = (1 − a)x_ss/b

Implement `target_ss(a, b, c, d, ysp)` → [x_ss, u_ss].
ch05.starter:
def target_ss(a, b, c, d, ysp):
    pass

ch05.title: Scalar Detectability
ch05.prompt:
A scalar mode is detectable if it is stable (|a| < 1) or seen at the output (c ≠ 0).

Implement `is_detectable_scalar(a, c)` → bool.
ch05.solution:
def is_detectable_scalar(a, c):
    return abs(a) < 1 or abs(c) > 1e-9

ch06.title: One Jacobi Iteration
ch06.prompt:
Distributed solvers iterate locally. The Jacobi update for A x = b holds every other component fixed:

    xᵢ⁺ = (bᵢ − Σⱼ≠ᵢ Aᵢⱼ xⱼ)/Aᵢᵢ

Implement `jacobi_step(A, b, x)` (new full vector).
ch06.solution:
def jacobi_step(A, b, x):
    n = len(b)
    return [(b[i] - sum(A[i][j]*x[j]
            for j in range(n) if j != i))/A[i][i]
            for i in range(n)]

ch06.title: Gauss-Seidel Iteration
ch06.prompt:
Gauss-Seidel is like Jacobi but reuses components already updated this sweep (in index order).

Implement `gauss_seidel_step(A, b, x)` returning the swept vector.

ch06.title: Cooperative Convex Combination
ch06.prompt:
Cooperative MPC updates plans by a convex step between the old and candidate plan:

    x = (1 − w)·x_old + w·x_new   (elementwise, 0 ≤ w ≤ 1)

Implement `convex_combine(xold, xnew, w)`.

ch06.title: Cooperative (Weighted) Cost
ch06.prompt:
A cooperative controller optimizes a weighted sum of local costs J = Σᵢ wᵢ Jᵢ.

Implement `cooperative_cost(costs, weights)`.

ch06.title: Consensus Averaging Step
ch06.prompt:
Subsystem i moves toward the average of itself and its neighbors:

    vᵢ⁺ = mean(vᵢ, {vⱼ : j ∈ neighbors})

Implement `consensus_step(vals, i, neighbors)`.

ch06.title: M-Player Average
ch06.prompt:
A simple cooperative target is the mean of all players' proposals.

Implement `player_average(vals)`.

ch06.title: Coupled Subsystem Dynamics
ch06.prompt:
Interconnected subsystems couple through A: (Ax)ᵢ = Σⱼ Aᵢⱼ xⱼ.

Implement `coupled_dynamics(A, x)` (matrix-vector product).

ch06.title: Convergence Check (∞-norm)
ch06.prompt:
Distributed iterations stop when max|aᵢ − bᵢ| falls below a tolerance.

Implement `max_diff(a, b)`.
ch06.solution:
def max_diff(a, b):
    return max(abs(a[i]-b[i]) for i in range(len(a)))

ch07.title: Which Critical Region?
ch07.prompt:
Explicit MPC partitions the state into regions. Given ordered bounds [lo, hi), return the index of the region containing x, or −1.

Implement `find_region(bounds, x)`.

ch07.title: Scalar QP with Bounds
ch07.prompt:
The parametric QP min ½ q u² + c u s.t. u_min ≤ u ≤ u_max has unconstrained optimum u* = −c/q, then clipped to the bounds (q > 0).

Implement `qp_scalar(q, c, umin, umax)`.
ch07.starter:
def qp_scalar(q, c, umin, umax):
    pass

ch07.title: Explicit LQR with Saturation
ch07.prompt:
Inside the unconstrained region the explicit law is u = −Kx, then clipped to the input limits.

Implement `explicit_lqr(k, x, umin, umax)`.
ch07.starter:
def explicit_lqr(k, x, umin, umax):
    pass

ch07.title: Evaluate a PWA Control Law
ch07.prompt:
The explicit solution is piecewise affine. Each region is (lo, hi, gain, offset); on lo ≤ x < hi, u = gain·x + offset.

Implement `eval_pwa(regions, x)`.

ch07.title: Piecewise-Quadratic Value Function
ch07.prompt:
The optimal value function is piecewise quadratic. Each region is (lo, hi, a, b, c); on lo ≤ x < hi, V = a x² + b x + c.

Implement `eval_pwq(regions, x)`.

ch07.title: Point in a Polyhedral Region
ch07.prompt:
Each critical region is {x : H x ≤ g}. x lies in it iff every row satisfies Hᵢ·x ≤ gᵢ.

Implement `in_region(H, g, x)` → bool.

ch07.title: Saturate the Explicit Control
ch07.prompt:
The explicit law must respect input limits: clamp u into [umin, umax].

Implement `saturate(u, umin, umax)`.
ch07.solution:
def saturate(u, umin, umax):
    return max(umin, min(umax, u))

ch08.title: Explicit Euler Step
ch08.prompt:
Direct methods discretize the dynamics. For ẋ = a x + b u the explicit-Euler step over dt is

    x⁺ = x + dt·(a x + b u)

Implement `euler_step(x, u, a, b, dt)`.
ch08.starter:
def euler_step(x, u, a, b, dt):
    pass
ch08.solution:
def euler_step(x, u, a, b, dt):
    return x + dt*(a*x + b*u)

ch08.title: Midpoint (RK2) Step
ch08.prompt:
The explicit midpoint method for ẋ = a·x is

    k₁ = a·x,   x⁺ = x + dt·a·(x + dt/2·k₁)

Implement `rk2_step(x, a, dt)`.

ch08.title: Runge-Kutta 4 Step
ch08.prompt:
RK4 for ẋ = a·x over dt:

    k₁=f(x), k₂=f(x+dt/2·k₁), k₃=f(x+dt/2·k₂), k₄=f(x+dt·k₃)
    x⁺ = x + dt/6·(k₁ + 2k₂ + 2k₃ + k₄),   f(s)=a·s

Implement `rk4_step(x, a, dt)`.
ch08.solution:
def rk4_step(x, a, dt):
    f = lambda s: a*s
    k1 = f(x)
    k2 = f(x + dt/2*k1)
    k3 = f(x + dt/2*k2)
    k4 = f(x + dt*k3)
    return x + dt/6*(k1 + 2*k2 + 2*k3 + k4)

ch08.title: Implicit Euler Step (Stiff)
ch08.prompt:
For stiff systems the implicit (backward) Euler step for ẋ = a·x solves x⁺ = x + dt·a·x⁺, i.e.

    x⁺ = x / (1 − dt·a)

Implement `implicit_euler(x, a, dt)`.
ch08.solution:
def implicit_euler(x, a, dt):
    return x/(1 - dt*a)

ch08.title: Newton Step
ch08.prompt:
Newton's method for solving f(x) = 0 (the optimality conditions) updates x⁺ = x − f(x)/f′(x).

Implement `newton_step(x, fx, dfx)`.
ch08.solution:
def newton_step(x, fx, dfx):
    return x - fx/dfx

ch08.title: Newton Iteration for √s
ch08.prompt:
Applying Newton's method to x² − s = 0 gives the iteration x ← ½(x + s/x). Run it n times from x₀ = s.

Implement `newton_sqrt(s, n)`.

ch08.title: Forward Finite Difference
ch08.prompt:
A one-sided derivative estimate is f′(x) ≈ (f(x+dx) − f(x))/dx.

Given f0 = f(x), f1 = f(x+dx), implement `forward_diff(f0, f1, dx)`.
ch08.solution:
def forward_diff(f0, f1, dx):
    return (f1 - f0)/dx

ch08.title: Central Finite Difference
ch08.prompt:
The more accurate central difference is f′(x) ≈ (f(x+dx) − f(x−dx))/(2dx).

Given fm = f(x−dx), fp = f(x+dx), implement `central_diff(fm, fp, dx)`.
ch08.solution:
def central_diff(fm, fp, dx):
    return (fp - fm)/(2*dx)

ch08.title: Single-Shooting Rollout
ch08.prompt:
Sequential (single-shooting) methods simulate the whole trajectory from x0 and an input sequence (explicit Euler on ẋ = a x + b u), then optimize. Return the final state.

Implement `single_shooting(x0, us, a, b, dt)`.
ch08.starter:
def single_shooting(x0, us, a, b, dt):
    pass

ch08.title: Multiple-Shooting Defect
ch08.prompt:
Multiple shooting enforces continuity between segments by driving the defect to zero: the gap between the previous segment's end state and the next segment's start state.

Implement `defect(xa, xb)` = xa − xb.
ch08.solution:
def defect(xa, xb):
    return xa - xb

ch08.title: Gauss-Newton Normal Matrix
ch08.prompt:
The Gauss-Newton Hessian approximation is JᵀJ. For a gradient vector J (one residual), the normal matrix entry (i,j) is Jᵢ·Jⱼ.

Implement `normal_matrix(J)` returning the n×n outer product.
